newsArray.title: West Indies, Zimbabwe unable to return from India due to Middle East airspace closures
newsArray.description: (Reuters) – The West Indies and Zimbabwe teams said today they have delayed their return from India after their exit from the Twenty20 World Cup due to international airspace closures amid ongoing conflict in the Middle East.The article West Indies, Zimbabwe unable to return from India due to Middle East airspace closures appeared first on Stabroek News.
creator: staff editor

newsArray.title: Conflict casts doubt over Iran’s participation in World Cup
newsArray.description: Iran secured qualification for its fourth consecutive World Cup appearance and was drawn into Group G alongside Belgium, Egypt, and New Zealand. The escalating war in the Middle East has delivered a major blow to the prospects of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, with Iran’s national soccer team, Team Melli, now facing serious uncertainty about whether it will even travel to North America for the tournament this summer.
creator: jerusalem post

newsArray.title: 'Trying to keep my family safe': Jonny Bairstow's desperate plea from war-hit Dubai
newsArray.description: Wicket-keeper Jonny Bairstow is stranded in Dubai with his family due to halted commercial flights amid rising Middle East tensions. He expressed distress over ensuring their safety and seeking a route home, while cricket schedules have also been disrupted. The UK government advises British nationals in the UAE to shelter in place.
creator: willow sports
